openmeter/ledger/historical/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL implementation of ledgerhistorical.Repo: books double-entry transactions (LedgerTransaction + LedgerEntry rows), manages transaction groups, sums entries, and lists transactions with cursor-based pagination. All reads must eagerly load SubAccount→Account→Route edges via WithSubAccount(WithAccount, WithRoute) to hydrate domain objects without N+1 queries.

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: repo.go declares `var _ ledgerhistorical.Repo = (*repo)(nil)` at package level. Every new adapter struct must include this compile-time guard.

patterns.name: Eager edge loading on every query
patterns.description: Every query that returns a hydrated Transaction must call WithEntries(func(eq){eq.WithSubAccount(func(sq){sq.WithAccount(); sq.WithRoute()})}) or hydrateHistoricalTransaction will error on missing edges.

patterns.name: hydrateHistoricalTransaction for domain reconstruction
patterns.description: All DB rows are converted to domain objects via hydrateHistoricalTransaction, which calls ledgerhistorical.NewTransactionFromData. Never map DB structs to domain objects inline; always go through this function.
patterns.example: items, err := slicesx.MapWithErr(dbItems, func(tx *db.LedgerTransaction) (*ledgerhistorical.Transaction, error) { return hydrateHistoricalTransaction(tx) })

patterns.name: Tri-tuple cursor pagination (bookedAt, createdAt, ID)
patterns.description: Pagination uses a three-field (BookedAt DESC, CreatedAt DESC, ID DESC) ordering with SQL predicate-based cursors. Both forward (Cursor) and backward (Before) directions are supported; Before results are slices.Reverse'd after fetch. NextCursor always points to the last returned item.

patterns.name: sumEntriesQuery builder for aggregation
patterns.description: SumEntries delegates to sumEntriesQuery.Build(client) which assembles entry predicates from ledger.Query. Add new filter dimensions by extending entryPredicates() and subAccountPredicates() in sumentries_query.go, not inline in repo methods.
patterns.example: q := sumEntriesQuery{query: query}; entryQuery, err := q.Build(r.db)

patterns.name: Tx via HijackTx + entutils.NewTxDriver
patterns.description: repo.Tx hijacks the connection via r.db.HijackTx and wraps it with entutils.NewTxDriver. Do not use db.BeginTx or manual transaction management; callers use pkg/framework/transaction.Driver.
patterns.example: txCtx, rawConfig, eDriver, err := r.db.HijackTx(ctx, &stdsql.TxOptions{ReadOnly: false}); return txCtx, entutils.NewTxDriver(eDriver, rawConfig), nil

patterns.name: Bulk entry creation after transaction row
patterns.description: BookTransaction first saves the LedgerTransaction row, then calls CreateBulk for all LedgerEntry rows in a single batch. Never insert entries one-by-one in a loop.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the repo struct holding *db.Client, the NewRepo constructor, the Tx method, and the interface compliance assertion. Start here when adding new repo methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not store additional state on repo beyond *db.Client; inject dependencies via constructor.

key_file_guides.role: Contains BookTransaction, CreateTransactionGroup, GetTransactionGroup, SumEntries, ListTransactions, and all cursor/ordering helpers. Core read/write operations live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: hydrateHistoricalTransaction panics if SubAccount→Account or SubAccount→Route edges are not loaded; always eager-load them in every query.

key_file_guides.role: Encapsulates the sumEntriesQuery builder with entryPredicates() and subAccountPredicates(). Also exposes SQL() for raw SQL output (used in view generation tooling).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Route filters call b.query.Filters.Route.Normalize() — normalization errors must propagate as ledger.ErrLedgerQueryInvalid, not a raw error.

key_file_guides.role: In-package DB integration tests using NewTestEnv(t) + env.DBSchemaMigrate(t). Tests cover pagination (forward, backward, no-overlap), filters (currency, creditMovement, annotations), and BookTransaction invariants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests call time.Sleep between transactions to ensure distinct CreatedAt timestamps for ordering — do not remove these sleeps.

anti_patterns: Loading edges lazily or omitting WithSubAccount/WithAccount/WithRoute — hydrateHistoricalTransaction will return an error on missing edges
Inline DB-to-domain mapping instead of routing through hydrateHistoricalTransaction
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-provided ctx in any repo method
Inserting LedgerEntry rows one-by-one instead of using CreateBulk
Adding filter logic directly inside ListTransactions or SumEntries instead of extending sumEntriesQuery or a dedicated predicate builder

decisions.decision: Cursor uses three fields (bookedAt, createdAt, ID) rather than a single auto-increment offset
decisions.rationale: BookedAt is the user-visible ordering field and can repeat; tie-breaking on createdAt+ID makes pagination stable across concurrent inserts without requiring a serial primary key.

decisions.decision: sumEntriesQuery is a separate struct with a Build method and a SQL() method
decisions.rationale: Isolates filter predicate logic from repo methods and enables the viewgen tool to extract raw SQL without executing a query.

decisions.decision: Tx uses HijackTx + entutils.NewTxDriver instead of db.BeginTx
decisions.rationale: Aligns with the project-wide entutils.TransactingRepo pattern so callers can bind the transaction via ctx; avoids leaking raw *sql.Tx across package boundaries.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new filtered query method that sums entries scoped by a new dimension
